engineGuide.h2: Nissan Micra Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Renault-sourced diesel powering K12/K13 Micras, known for fuel economy but injector sensitivity.
compatibleVariants: K12 1.5 dCi Visia, K12 1.5 dCi Acenta, K13 1.5 dCi Acenta, K13 1.5 dCi Tekna
commonFailures: Injector failure causing black smoke and rough running (80k+ miles)
Turbo oil seal leaks leading to power loss
EGR valve clogging on short-journey vehicles

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: Base K11 engine, simple and robust but prone to oil consumption at high mileage.
compatibleVariants: K11 1.0 Visia, K11 1.0 Base, K11 1.0 GX
commonFailures: Oil pump wear leading to low oil pressure (100k+ miles)
Head gasket failure after overheating events
Water pump failure causing coolant loss

commonFailures: Head gasket failure following water pump issues (90k+ miles)
Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattling noise
Oil consumption on high-mileage units

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol

commonFailures: Head gasket failure after overheating

Coolant leaks from water pump housing

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol

compatibleVariants: K12 1.2 S, K12 1.2 SE, K12 1.2 Visia, K12 1.2 Acenta
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and tensioner failure (8k-60k miles)
Oil consumption from piston ring wear
Cold start rattle indicating chain wear

Coolant leaks from thermostat housing

entries.history: K13's efficient three-cylinder, improved reliability over CR series but watch water pump.

Timing chain stretch on neglected units

commonProblems.h2: Nissan Micra Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan Micra's CG, CR and HR petrol engines dominate UK replacement demand, with K12 CR12DE timing chain failures and K11 CG head gasket issues driving most enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - Why K12 CR Series Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Micra K12 1.2 S, 1.2 SE, 1.4 S, 1.4 SE (2002-2010, CR12DE & CR14DE)
problems.rootCause: Poor oil quality or extended service intervals cause acidic oil to attack the timing chain tensioner, allowing chain stretch and potential valve damage.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain Replacement - Replace chain, guides, tensioner before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove timing cover, fit new chain kit, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure if caught early; viable before rattling starts

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old engine, install rebuilt unit with upgraded timing components, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when chain has already damaged valves; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your K12 Micra has cold-start rattling but still runs, a preventative timing chain kit from a specialist offers the best value. If the chain has snapped or jumped timing, replacement with a rebuilt CR12DE/CR14DE engine is more cost-effective than repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CR12DE engine prices for your Nissan Micra K12

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Why K11 CG Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Micra K11 1.0 Visia, 1.3 GX, 1.4 SR (1992-2002, CG10DE, CG13DE, CGA3DE)
problems.rootCause: Water pump failure or coolant neglect leads to overheating, warping the cylinder head and blowing the head gasket.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Head Skim & Gasket - Resurface head, replace gasket and water pump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, machine surface, fit new gasket, water pump and belts
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt CG unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old engine, install rebuilt CG13DE/CG10DE with new head gasket and water pump
repairOptions.longevity: Best when block is warped or cracked; includes warranty and upgraded components

problems.recommendation: For K11 Micras valued under £1,500, a rebuilt CG13DE engine from £450 offers better long-term value than a head repair, especially if the block shows signs of overheating damage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CG13DE Nissan Micra engine replacement

problems.h4: Water Pump Failure - The Silent Killer on High-Mileage Micras
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Micra K11/K12/K13 CG13DE, CR12DE, HR12DE (1992-2017)
problems.rootCause: Original equipment water pumps use plastic impellers that degrade over time, causing coolant loss and overheating.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Water Pump Replacement - Replace pump, thermostat, coolant
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain coolant, remove timing cover, fit new pump and thermostat, refill
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative maintenance; recommended at 60k miles or with any coolant leak

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - If overheating caused damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace entire engine if head gasket or block damaged by overheating
repairOptions.longevity: Only needed if water pump failure was ignored; rebuilt units include new pump

problems.recommendation: Replace the water pump proactively at 60k miles on any Micra. If you've had overheating, get a compression test before deciding between head repair or full engine replacement.
problems.cta: Compare Nissan Micra water pump and engine replacement costs

problems.h4: Diesel Injector Failure - K9K 1.5 dCi Common Issue
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Micra K12/K13 1.5 dCi Acenta, Tekna (2007-2017, K9K)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure diesel injectors wear internally, causing poor atomisation, black smoke, and rough running.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Injector Replacement - Replace faulty injectors, code to ECU
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, fit reconditioned or new units, code to engine management
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block and turbo are healthy; requires specialist diagnostic equipment

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt K9K Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt diesel unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old K9K, install rebuilt unit with new injectors, turbo checked, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when multiple components are worn; includes warranty and upgraded internals

problems.recommendation: If your K9K Micra has black smoke but good compression, injector replacement from a diesel specialist is cost-effective. If the turbo or bottom end is also worn, a rebuilt K9K engine offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K9K Nissan Micra diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Oil Pump Failure - High-Mileage CG Engine Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Micra K11 1.0/1.3/1.4 (1992-2002, CG10DE, CG13DE, CGA3DE)
problems.rootCause: Oil pump drive gears wear over time, reducing oil pressure and causing bearing wear or seizure.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Oil Pump Replacement - Replace pump, seals, filter
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove sump, replace oil pump and drive, refill with fresh oil
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before bearing damage; requires engine removal on some variants

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt CG Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install rebuilt CG13DE/CG10DE with new oil pump, bearings, and upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: Best when low oil pressure has caused bearing wear; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: If your CG-engine Micra has low oil pressure warning or knocking, replacement with a rebuilt unit is safer and often cheaper than attempting a bottom-end repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CG13DE engine prices for your Nissan Micra K11

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Micra
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with K12 timing chain rattle, K11 head gasket concerns, or K13 water pump worries, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Nissan Micra specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Micra Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Micra and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Nissan Micra 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K diesel powered later K12 and all K13 Micras, offering strong fuel economy for high-mileage drivers but requiring careful servicing to avoid injector and turbo issues.

items.title: Nissan Micra 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.0L CG10DE powered base K11 Micras, a simple and affordable engine but prone to oil pump wear and head gasket issues on high-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump wear leading to low pressure
items.cta: Compare Nissan Micra 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Micra 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L CR12DE and HR12DE powered K12 and K13 Micras respectively; the CR12DE is notorious for timing chain failures while the HR12DE improved reliability but watch water pump.

items.title: Nissan Micra 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The popular 1.3L CG13DE was the sweet spot for K11 Micras, offering balanced performance and widespread availability of replacement units, though head gasket care is essential.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure after water pump issues
timing chain tensioner wear at high mileage

items.title: Nissan Micra 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L CGA3DE and CR14DE offered more power for late K11 and K12 Micras, sharing architecture with smaller CG/CR engines but with similar failure patterns to watch.

items.description: The 1.6L HR16DE powered sportier K13 Micras, a robust four-cylinder favoured by enthusiasts and fleet users, with turbo variants requiring attention to oil seals.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo oil seal leaks on boosted variants

engineYears.h2: Nissan Micra Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Nissan Micra's production timeline from 1982 to present, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: K11 Generation: CG Series Petrol Engines
years.description: The K11 Micra launched with simple, robust CG-series petrol engines that remain the most common replacement demand today.
keyChanges: CG10DE 1.0L and CG13DE 1.3L introduced as core engines
CGA3DE 1.4L added for late-model SR variants (2000)
Facelift updates 1998 with minor trim changes
knownFor: Simple, affordable engines with widespread breaker support
Head gasket failure common after water pump issues
Oil pump wear on high-mileage CG10DE units
Excellent parts availability and low replacement costs
years.cta: Get quotes for 1992-2002 Nissan Micra replacement engines
years.ticker: Nissan Micra K11 engine replacement • CG13DE head gasket failure • Nissan Micra 1.3 overheating fix

years.preview: K12 Generation: CR Series & K9K Diesel
years.description: The K12 introduced new CR-series petrol engines and the K9K diesel, but early CR12DE units suffer notorious timing chain failures.
keyChanges: K12 generation launched 2002 with all-new CR12DE 1.2L engine
K9K 1.5 dCi diesel introduced 2007 for economy-focused buyers
Facelift 2007 with updated styling and interior
knownFor: CR12DE timing chain stretch failures documented at 8,000-60,000 miles
Cold-start rattling is classic symptom of chain wear
K9K diesel offers strong economy but injector sensitivity
High UK replacement demand due to age and failure patterns
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2010 Nissan Micra replacement engines
years.ticker: Nissan Micra K12 timing chain cost • CR12DE engine replacement • K12 cold start rattle fix
enquiries: Nissan Micra K12 timing chain cost

years.preview: K13 Generation: HR Series Petrol & K9K Diesel
years.description: The K13 moved to a new platform with HR-series three-cylinder petrol engines, improving efficiency but requiring water pump attention.
keyChanges: K13 generation launched 2010 on new B-platform
HR12DE 1.2L three-cylinder petrol became core engine
HR16DE 1.6L four-cylinder offered for higher performance
K9K 1.5 dCi diesel continued from K12 with updates
Facelift 2014 with revised front end and interior tech
knownFor: HR12DE more reliable than CR12DE but watch water pump at 70k+ miles
HR16DE robust four-cylinder favoured by enthusiasts
K9K diesel continues with similar injector/turbo considerations
Growing replacement demand as K13s age out of warranty
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2017 Nissan Micra replacement engines
years.ticker: Nissan Micra K13 water pump replacement • HR12DE engine cost • K13 1.6 timing chain service
enquiries: Nissan Micra K13 water pump replacement

years.preview: Present - K15 Generation: HRA0 Turbo Petrol & MHEV
years.description: The current K15 Micra uses downsized turbo petrol engines with mild-hybrid assist, offering efficiency but requiring strict servicing discipline.
keyChanges: K15 generation launched 2017 on Renault-Nissan CMF-B platform
HRA0 1.0L IG-T turbo petrol introduced as core engine
12V mild-hybrid (MHEV) system added to select variants (2020)
No diesel option offered in UK market for K15
mainEngines: HRA0 - 1.0L turbo petrol (K15 0.9/1.0 IG-T all trims)
HRA0 MHEV - 1.0L turbo with 12V mild-hybrid assist (2020+)
knownFor: Strong fuel economy and low CO2 for company car appeal
Carbon buildup on intake valves requires walnut blasting service
Turbo actuator sticking can cause limp mode on early units
Lower replacement volume due to newer age but growing enquiries
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-present Nissan Micra replacement engines
years.ticker: Nissan Micra K15 turbo replacement • HRA0 carbon cleaning cost • K15 MHEV engine compatibility

faq.h2: Nissan Micra Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What engine does my Nissan Micra have?
items.answer: Your Nissan Micra's engine depends on its generation: K11 (1992-2002) used CG10DE/CG13DE/CGA3DE petrol; K12 (2002-2010) used CR12DE/CR14DE petrol and K9K diesel; K13 (2010-2017) used HR12DE/HR16DE petrol and K9K diesel; K15 (2017-present) uses HRA0 turbo petrol.
keyPoints: Check your V5C logbook for engine code
K12 CR12DE engines prone to timing chain issues
Enter registration above for instant engine identification
Engine code stamped on block near gearbox
items.cta: Compare Nissan Micra engine prices by code

items.question: How much does a Nissan Micra timing chain replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A preventative timing chain replacement on a Nissan Micra K12 CR12DE costs £500-£900 from a specialist, but if the chain has failed and damaged valves, a rebuilt engine from £550 is often better value.
keyPoints: CR12DE chain failures documented at 8,000-60,000 miles
Cold-start rattling indicates chain wear needing attention
Rebuilt CR12DE engines include upgraded timing components
Get multiple quotes to compare repair vs replace options
items.cta: Get quotes for CR12DE Nissan Micra engine replacement

items.question: Is the Nissan Micra engine reliable?
items.answer: Nissan Micra reliability varies by generation: K11 CG engines are simple but head gasket prone; K12 CR engines suffer timing chain issues if poorly serviced; K13 HR engines are more robust; K15 turbo units need strict servicing.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes critical for CR12DE timing chain life
K11 head gasket issues often follow water pump failure
K13 HR12DE improved reliability but watch water pump
Overall, Micras are affordable to repair with good parts availability
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Nissan Micra engine options

items.question: What causes Nissan Micra K12 timing chain failure?
items.answer: Nissan Micra K12 CR12DE timing chain failures are caused by acidic oil from extended service intervals attacking the tensioner, allowing chain stretch that can jump timing and damage valves.
keyPoints: Use high-quality 5W-30 oil and change every 10k miles max
Cold-start rattling is early warning sign of chain wear
Preventative chain kit replacement costs £500-£900
If valves are damaged, rebuilt engine from £550 is cost-effective
items.cta: Find CR12DE timing chain replacement specialists UK

items.question: Will a Nissan Cube engine fit my Micra?
items.answer: Yes, Nissan Cube models sharing the CG13DE 1.3L engine (2002-2008) are compatible donor engines for K11 Micra 1.3 variants, but always verify engine code and mounting points before purchase.
keyPoints: CG13DE Cube engines match K11 Micra 1.3 specifications
Confirm ECU compatibility and wiring harness requirements
Breaker-sourced Cube engines often lower mileage than Micra donors
Enter your registration to verify exact compatibility
items.cta: Check Nissan Cube CG13DE engine availability for Micra

items.question: How much does a Nissan Micra engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Nissan Micra engine replacement costs range from £400 for a used CG10DE to £1,900 for a rebuilt HRA0 turbo, with most common CR12DE rebuilds from £550-£1,200 supply-only from UK specialists.
keyPoints: Used engines cheapest but limited warranty (3-6 months)
Rebuilt engines offer best value with 12-month warranty
Supply & fit adds £300-£600 labour depending on location
Compare multiple quotes to find best price for your variant
items.cta: Compare Nissan Micra engine replacement prices now

items.question: Is the Nissan Micra petrol or diesel?
items.answer: Nissan Micra has been offered with both petrol and diesel engines in the UK: petrol dominated K11-K13, while diesel K9K 1.5 dCi was available on K12/K13; K15 is petrol-only with turbo and MHEV options.
keyPoints: K11 (1992-2002): Petrol only (CG series)
K12/K13 (2002-2017): Petrol and K9K 1.5 dCi diesel
K15 (2017-present): Petrol turbo and MHEV only
Check your fuel cap or V5C to confirm your Micra's fuel type
items.cta: Get quotes for your Nissan Micra fuel type engine

items.question: How long does a Nissan Micra engine last?
items.answer: With proper servicing, Nissan Micra engines can last 100,000-150,000 miles; CG13DE and HR12DE are most durable, while CR12DE timing chain issues can cause premature failure if oil changes are neglected.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes every 10k miles critical for chain life
Replace water pump proactively at 60k-70k miles
Address coolant leaks immediately to prevent head gasket damage
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components can exceed original lifespan
items.cta: Find long-life rebuilt Nissan Micra engines

items.question: Can I rebuild my Nissan Micra CG13DE engine?
items.answer: Yes, Nissan Micra CG13DE engines can be rebuilt, and for K11 models valued under £1,500, a rebuilt CG13DE from £450 often offers better value than repair, especially if head gasket or block damage is present.
keyPoints: CG13DE rebuilds include new head gasket, seals and bearings
Upgraded water pump recommended to prevent repeat failure
Rebuilt units come with 12-month warranty vs 3-6 months on repair
Compare rebuild quotes against full engine replacement costs
items.cta: Compare CG13DE rebuild vs replacement prices

items.question: Where can I buy a Nissan Micra engine in the UK?
items.answer: Nissan Micra engines are widely available from UK breakers, specialist rebuilders and online marketplaces; EnginesMarket compares prices from 100+ vetted specialists to find the best deal for your variant and budget.
keyPoints: CG13DE most common and affordable due to K11 volume
CR12DE rebuilt units include upgraded timing components
K9K diesel engines require specialist diesel knowledge
Enter registration for instant compatibility check and quotes
items.cta: Get free Nissan Micra engine quotes from UK specialists
